title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Data-System Transfer
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab support system has 3 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.

Keeping every field forever is the safest data policy. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Data Custodianship
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-data-custodianship-1cbv95" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; database-system and data-lifecycle evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Data Custodianship.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Data Custodianship into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Data Custodianship, keep Data Custodianship connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-data-custodianship-1cbv95
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT name, type
FROM sqlite_schema
WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
ORDER BY type, name;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
